zh.Urach, Baden, Germany: 巴登阿赤 斯塔特武拉賀
zh.lifeSummary: Ulrich III. Graf  Neuenburg zu Nidau是Ulric III de Neuchâtel和Bertha von Grenchen的兒子，於1175年出生在瑞士波格a特爾。他於大約1196年與Jolanthe Gräfin von Urach Arberg結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1225年12月31日享年50歲。

zh-hans.Urach, Baden, Germany: 巴登阿赤 斯塔特武拉賀
zh-hans.lifeSummary: Ulrich III. Graf  Neuenburg zu Nidau是Ulric III de Neuchâtel和Bertha von Grenchen的儿子，于1175年出生在瑞士波格a特爾。他于大约1196年与Jolanthe Gräfin von Urach Arberg结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他去世于1225年12月31日享年50岁。

nameMeanings.name: Graf
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Kurt, Otto, Erwin, Ernst, Fritz, Gerhard, Hermann, Horst, Manfred, Udo, Alois.

German (also Gräf): status name from Middle High German grāve, grābe, which was used as a title denoting various more or less aristocratic dignitaries and officials. In later times it became established as a title of nobility equivalent to the Romance count. The vocabulary word also denoted a variety of different minor local functionaries in different parts of Germany. In the Grand Duchy of Hesse, for example, it was used for the holder of the comparatively humble office of village headman (compare Mayer , Schulz , and Vogt ). The surname could have originated from any of these senses or be a metonymic occupational or status name for a servant or retainer of a count, or a nickname for someone who gave himself airs and graces. This surname is also found in many other European countries, for example in France (Alsace and Lorraine), Hungary, Poland, Czechia, Slovakia, Croatia, and Slovenia, often as a translation into German of the corresponding Hungarian and Slavic surnames Gróf and Grof . Compare Graef .
Jewish (Ashkenazic): artificial name selected, like   Herzog   and other words denoting titles, because of their aristocratic connotations.

variants.name: Grebe
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Erwin, Fritz, Hans, Willi.
German (Hesse): variant of   Graf  .

variants.name: De Graaf
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Dutch Gerrit, Adriaan, Bastiaan, Cornelis, Dirk, Geert.

Dutch: from graaf ‘count’ (see Graf ), with the addition of the definite article de ‘the’, hence a nickname for the servant of a count or for someone supposedly resembling one, and also a topographic or habitational name referring to a house or inn named with this word.

variants.name: Graefe
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Armin, Arno, Goetz, Horst, Johannes.
German (North Rhine-Westphalia; also Gräfe): variant of Graf 1.

variants.name: Grefe
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Erhardt, Gerhard, Gerhart, Kurt.
North German: variant of   Graf  .

variants.name: Greve
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Gerhard, Otto, Reinhold, Bernhardt, Claus, Erna, Erwin, Ewald, Fritz, Kurt.
North German and Scandinavian: status name from Middle Low German and Danish greve ‘count’, equivalent to German Graf .
Dutch: vowel variant of Grave ‘count’, or a topographic name from Middle Dutch greve ‘ditch, moat, channel’.

variants.name: Croff
meanings: Altered form of German   Graf   or   Graff  . Or of Dutch   Kroft  .

variants.name: Mayer
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Otto, Hans, Franz, Helmut, Wilhelm, Erwin, Gerhard, Heinz, Ilse, Lorenz, Wolfgang.

English (Staffordshire): post-medieval form of Mear, which is either a topographic name, from Middle English mere ‘pond, pool’ (Old English mere) or Middle English mere ‘boundary’ (Old English (ge)mǣre), or a habitational name from one or more of the many places with names derived from these terms, such as Mere (Cheshire, Lincolnshire, Wiltshire), Meir in Caverswall (Staffordshire), and Meare (Somerset).
English: occupational name for a physician, from Old French mire, Middle English mire, meir, meyre, originally synonymous with Myer .

variants.name: Grafe
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Fritz, Ralf.
German (also Gräfe): variant of Graf 1.

variants.name: Graef
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Egon, Lothar, Otto, Armin, Claus, Erwin, Franz, Friedrich, Hans, Helmut, Hermann, Jochen.
German (also Gräf): see Graf .
Dutch: variant of Graaf, see De Graaf .

variants.name: Graff
meanings: German and Jewish (Ashkenazic) (also Gräff): variant of Graf . Compare Graeff .

English (London): metonymic occupational name for a clerk or scribe, from Old French grafe ‘stylus, pencil’, a derivative of Old French grafer ‘to write’ (from Late Latin grafare, from Greek graphein). It is uncertain whether the English name survived the medieval period or whether the early 17th century bearers in Britain were in fact of German origin.

nameMeanings.name: zu
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 祖, meaning ‘ancestors’ in Chinese: (i) from the first element of the personal names Zu Jia (祖甲), Zu Yi (祖乙), Zu Xin (祖辛), Zu Ding (祖丁), and Zu Geng (祖庚), personal names of several kings during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). (ii) from the first element of the personal names of Zu Ji (祖己) and Zu Yin (祖尹), who were descendants of an official called Zhong Hui who lived some time during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ).

Mandarin form of the surname 俎, meaning ‘sacrificial utensil’ in ancient Chinese: (i) adopted as a surname by the Bao Zu (鮑俎) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (ii) said to be adopted by the fourth son of the uncle of the famous general Yue Fei (1103–1142  AD ), who changed the original surname Zhu (祝) to Zu (俎) in order to escape from political persecutions.

variants.name: Tsu
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chen, Chi, Ching, Kin, Kung, Kuo, Yuk.  Vietnamese Mui, Loc.
Chinese:
alternative Mandarin forms of the Chinese surnames 祖 and 俎, see   Zu  .

nameMeanings.name: Nidau
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Americanized form of German   Nida  .

variants.name: Nida
meanings: German: variant of Nidda, a habitational name from Nidda, a town (also a river) in Hesse. In some cases the surname was run together with the preposition von ‘from, of’, giving rise to the form Vonada in the US and Vonnieda in Germany. The surnames Nida and Nidda are rare in Germany. Compare Niday .
Polish: unexplained.

nameMeanings.name: Ulrich
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: from uodal ‘riches’ + rīc ‘power’; the name of two major German saints, Ulrich of Augsburg ( d. 973 ) and Ulrich of Cluny ( c.1018–93 ), and the Blessed Ulrich of Einsiedeln in Switzerland ( d. c.980 ).

nameMeanings.name: III.
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Japanese Yuko, Aiko, Hideaki, Hiroki, Hiroyuki, Isamu, Kaoru, Katsumi, Keiichiro, Kenji, Masako, Masayuki.
Japanese: variously written, sometimes with characters used phonetically; the most probable meanings are 仁井 ‘benevolent well’, 新居 ‘new residence’, or 新井 ‘new well’. It is not common in Japan.

en.lifeSummary: Ulrich III. Graf  Neuenburg zu Nidau was born in 1175, in Neuchâtel, Neuchâtel, Switzerland as the son of Ulric III de Neuchâtel and Bertha von Grenchen. He married Jolanthe Gräfin von Urach Arberg about 1196. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 3 daughters. He died on 31 December 1225, at the age of 50.

fr.Corcelles, Neuchâtel, Neuchâtel, Switzer: Corcelles, Neuchâtel, Canton de Neuchâtel, Suisse
fr.lifeSummary: Ulrich III. Graf  Neuenburg zu Nidau est né en 1175, à Neuchâtel, Canton de Neuchâtel, Suisse, fils de Ulric III de Neuchâtel et Bertha von Grenchen. Il a épousé Jolanthe Gräfin von Urach Arberg environ 1196. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 3 filles. Il est décédé le 31 décembre 1225, à l’âge de 50 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Ulrich III. Graf  Neuenburg zu Nidau wurde 1175 in Neuchâtel, Neuenburg, Schweiz, als Sohn von Ulric III de Neuchâtel und Bertha von Grenchen geboren. Er hat um 1196 Jolanthe Gräfin von Urach Arberg geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Er starb am 31. Dezember 1225 im Alter von 50 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Ulrich III. Graf  Neuenburg zu Nidau è nato il nel 1175 nel Neuchâtel, Neuchâtel, Svizzera come figlio di Ulric III de Neuchâtel e Bertha von Grenchen. Lui sposato Jolanthe Gräfin von Urach Arberg circa 1196. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 3 figlie. Morì il 31 dicembre 1225, all'età di 50 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Ulrich III. Graf  Neuenburg zu Nidauは1175年にスイスネウエンブルグネウチaテルでUlric III de NeuchâtelとBertha von Grenchenの息子として生まれた。約1196年にJolanthe Gräfin von Urach Arbergと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は1225年12月31日に50歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Ulrich III. Graf  Neuenburg zu Nidau(은)는 1175년 스위스 니우버그 니우cha텔에서 Ulric III de Neuchâtel(와)과 Bertha von Grenchen의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 대략 1196년 Jolanthe Gräfin von Urach Arberg(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1225년 12월 31일 50세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Ulrich III. Graf  Neuenburg zu Nidau nasceu em 1175, em Neuchâtel, Neuenburg, Suiça filho de Ulric III de Neuchâtel e Bertha von Grenchen. Ele casou-se com Jolanthe Gräfin von Urach Arberg aproximadamente 1196. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 3 filhas. Ele faleceu em 31 de dezembro de 1225, com 50 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Ulrich III. Graf  Neuenburg zu Nidau родился в 1175 г. в местности неучaтел, неуенбург, Швейцария. Его родителей звали Ulric III de Neuchâtel и Bertha von Grenchen. Он женился на женщине по имени Jolanthe Gräfin von Urach Arberg около 1196 г. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 3 дочери. Он умер 31 декабря 1225 г. в возрасте 50 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Ulrich III. Graf  Neuenburg zu Nidau nació en 1175, en Neuchâtel, Neuenburg, Suiza como hijo de Ulric III de Neuchâtel y Bertha von Grenchen. Se casó con Jolanthe Gräfin von Urach Arberg aproximadamente 1196. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió el 31 de diciembre de 1225, a la edad de 50 años.
